message:
fix(http2): enforce stream ID rules, advertise max concurrent streams, cap HPACK table (bd-2c9t)

- Advertise SETTINGS_MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS=1 in server SETTINGS since
  streams are processed serially. Informs clients and enables detection
  of misbehaving connections.
- Validate client stream IDs are odd (RFC 7540 §5.1.1) and strictly
  monotonically increasing across all three HTTP/2 server paths.
- Cap HPACK dynamic table size to 64 KiB (MAX_HPACK_TABLE_SIZE) when
  processing peer SETTINGS_HEADER_TABLE_SIZE to prevent gradual memory
  exhaustion on long-lived connections.
- Add unit tests for SETTINGS payload structure and table size cap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
